# Almach

> A modern, accessible React UI component library monorepo.
> Built on React Aria Components, Tailwind CSS v4, TanStack Query, TanStack Form, and Zod.
> Maintained by [@rafaalrazzak](https://github.com/rafaalrazzak).

- Docs: https://almach.pages.dev
- GitHub: https://github.com/almach-lab/base
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/org/almach

## Packages

| Package | Version | Description |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| `@almach/ui` | 1.2.1 | 35+ accessible components (React Aria Components + Tailwind CSS v4) |
| `@almach/forms` | 3.0.0 | Type-safe form builder (TanStack Form + Zod) |
| `@almach/query` | 1.0.0 | Query/mutation factories (TanStack Query v5) |
| `@almach/utils` | ŌĆö | Tree-shakable utilities and TypeScript type helpers |

---

## Installation

```bash
# Install individual packages
bun add @almach/ui
bun add @almach/forms
bun add @almach/query
bun add @almach/utils

# Or install everything
bun add @almach/ui @almach/forms @almach/query @almach/utils
```

### Requirements

- React 18+
- Tailwind CSS v4
- Bun (recommended) or npm/pnpm/yarn

---

## Setup

### 1. Import global styles

In your app entry point or layout file, import the stylesheet from `@almach/ui`:

```ts
import "@almach/ui/styles";
```

This imports CSS variables and the Tailwind v4 theme. You must do this before using any components.

### 2. Wrap your app with providers

```tsx
import { BasedQueryProvider } from "@almach/query";
import { Toaster } from "@almach/ui";

export function App({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <BasedQueryProvider>
      {children}
      <Toaster />
    </BasedQueryProvider>
  );
}
```

`BasedQueryProvider` sets up TanStack Query with sensible defaults (stale time: 1 min, GC time: 5 min, retry 3x except on 4xx). `Toaster` is required for `toast()` notifications.

### 3. Tailwind CSS v4 config

No `tailwind.config.js` needed. Just point Tailwind at the source:

```css
/* app.css */
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "@almach/ui/styles";
```

---

## Theming

All design tokens are CSS variables ŌĆö override them in your stylesheet:

```css
:root {
  /* Brand color (HSL values without hsl()) */
  --primary:             43 90% 44%;
  --primary-foreground:  0 0% 100%;

  /* Surfaces */
  --background:          0 0% 100%;
  --foreground:          240 10% 3.9%;
  --card:                0 0% 100%;
  --card-foreground:     240 10% 3.9%;
  --popover:             0 0% 100%;
  --popover-foreground:  240 10% 3.9%;

  /* Neutral */
  --secondary:           240 4.8% 95.9%;
  --secondary-foreground: 240 5.9% 10%;
  --muted:               240 4.8% 95.9%;
  --muted-foreground:    240 3.8% 46.1%;
  --accent:              240 4.8% 95.9%;
  --accent-foreground:   240 5.9% 10%;

  /* Semantic */
  --destructive:         0 84.2% 60.2%;
  --destructive-foreground: 0 0% 98%;
  --success:             142 76% 36%;
  --success-foreground:  0 0% 100%;
  --warning:             38 92% 50%;
  --warning-foreground:  0 0% 100%;

  /* Controls */
  --border:              240 5.9% 90%;
  --input:               240 5.9% 90%;
  --ring:                43 90% 44%;

  /* Border radius */
  --radius:              0.625rem;
}

.dark {
  --background: 240 10% 3.9%;
  /* ... override dark tokens */
}
```

---

## @almach/ui

### Button

```tsx
import { Button } from "@almach/ui";

<Button>Click me</Button>
<Button variant="outline" size="sm">Small</Button>
<Button loading>Saving...</Button>
<Button leftIcon={<SaveIcon />}>Save</Button>
<Button rightIcon={<ArrowRightIcon />}>Next</Button>
<Button asChild><a href="/dashboard">Go</a></Button>
```

**Props:**

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `variant` | `"default" \| "destructive" \| "outline" \| "secondary" \| "ghost" \| "link" \| "success" \| "warning"` | `"default"` | Visual style |
| `size` | `"sm" \| "default" \| "lg" \| "icon" \| "icon-sm" \| "icon-lg"` | `"default"` | Size |
| `loading` | `boolean` | `false` | Shows spinner, disables button |
| `leftIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | ŌĆö | Icon before children |
| `rightIcon` | `React.ReactNode` | ŌĆö | Icon after children |
| `asChild` | `boolean` | `false` | Render as child element (slot pattern) |

---

### Input

```tsx
import { Input } from "@almach/ui";

<Input placeholder="Email" type="email" />
<Input leftElement={<SearchIcon />} placeholder="Search" />
<Input rightElement={<EyeIcon />} type="password" />
<Input error placeholder="Invalid field" />

// Date sub-component
<Input.Date value={date} onChange={setDate} />
<Input.Date withCalendar format="DD/MM/YYYY" value={date} onChange={setDate} />

// Currency sub-component
<Input.Currency />
<Input.Currency defaultCurrency="EUR" />
<Input.Currency value={{ amount: 1234.56, currency: "EUR" }} onChange={setVal} />

// Custom flag renderer (CDN images, SVG libraries, etc.)
<Input.Currency
  renderFlag={(code) => (
    <img src={`https://flagcdn.com/w20/${code.toLowerCase()}.png`}
         width={20} height={15} alt={code} className="rounded-sm object-cover" />
  )}
/>
```

**Input Props:**

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `leftElement` | `React.ReactNode` | Icon/element on the left (non-interactive) |
| `rightElement` | `React.ReactNode` | Icon/element on the right |
| `error` | `boolean` | Applies destructive ring |

**Input.Date Props:**

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `Date \| undefined` | ŌĆö | Controlled date value |
| `onChange` | `(date: Date \| undefined) => void` | ŌĆö | Change handler |
| `format` | `string` | `"MM/DD/YYYY"` | Segment order. Tokens: `MM`, `DD`, `YYYY`. Separator = first non-token char. Examples: `"DD/MM/YYYY"`, `"YYYY-MM-DD"` |
| `withCalendar` | `boolean` | `false` | Show calendar popover button |
| `error` | `boolean` | `false` | Applies destructive ring |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables all segments |

**Input.Currency Props:**

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `value` | `{ amount: number \| null; currency: string }` | ŌĆö | Controlled value |
| `onChange` | `(value: { amount: number \| null; currency: string }) => void` | ŌĆö | Change handler |
| `defaultCurrency` | `string` | `"USD"` | Initial currency code (uncontrolled). Ignored when `value` is supplied |
| `currencies` | `CurrencyDef[]` | built-in 36 | Override the currency list. Set `popular: true` to show in Popular group |
| `renderFlag` | `(countryCode: string, currency: CurrencyDef) => ReactNode` | `<CurrencyFlagBadge />` | Custom flag renderer. See examples below. |
| `placeholder` | `string` | `"0.00"` | Amount field placeholder |
| `currencySelector` | `"editable" \| "readonly" \| "hidden"` | `"editable"` | `"editable"` = dropdown; `"readonly"` = static badge; `"hidden"` = selector removed |
| `error` | `boolean` | `false` | Applies destructive ring; sets `aria-invalid` |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Disables amount input and currency selector |
| `id` | `string` | ŌĆö | Forwarded to the inner `<input>` for `<Label htmlFor>` association |
| `className` | `string` | ŌĆö | Extra classes on the outer wrapper div |

`CurrencyDef`: `{ code: string; symbol: string; name: string; countryCode?: string; popular?: boolean }`

`CurrencySelectorMode`: `"editable" | "readonly" | "hidden"`

`countryCode` is ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (e.g. "US", "EU", "ID"). Used by `renderFlag`.

Built-in popular currencies: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, CHF, AUD, CAD, CNY. 28 additional currencies (INR, BRL, MXN, KRW, SGD, HKD, NOK, SEK, DKK, NZD, ZAR, TRY, AED, SAR, PLN, CZK, HUF, THB, IDR, MYR, PHP, VND, ILS, EGP, NGN, UAH, CLP, COP, PEN).

```tsx
import { CURRENCIES, CurrencyFlagBadge } from "@almach/ui";

// Set default currency (uncontrolled ŌĆö no value prop)
<Input.Currency defaultCurrency="EUR" />

// Controlled value
<Input.Currency value={{ amount: 1234.56, currency: "EUR" }} onChange={setVal} />

// Currency selector modes
<Input.Currency currencySelector="editable" />   // default ŌĆö user can open dropdown
<Input.Currency currencySelector="readonly" />   // fixed badge, no dropdown
<Input.Currency currencySelector="hidden" />     // only symbol + amount shown

// Restrict currency list
const limited = CURRENCIES.filter((c) => ["USD", "EUR", "GBP"].includes(c.code));
<Input.Currency currencies={limited} />

// CDN image flags (flagcdn.com)
<Input.Currency
  renderFlag={(code) => (
    <img src={`https://flagcdn.com/w20/${code.toLowerCase()}.png`}
         width={20} height={15} alt={code} className="rounded-sm object-cover" />
  )}
/>

// country-flag-icons SVG library (npm i country-flag-icons)
import Flags from "country-flag-icons/react/3x2";
<Input.Currency
  renderFlag={(code) => {
    const Flag = Flags[code as keyof typeof Flags];
    return Flag ? <Flag className="h-3.5 w-5 rounded-sm" /> : <CurrencyFlagBadge countryCode={code} />;
  }}
/>
```

---

### Badge

```tsx
import { Badge } from "@almach/ui";

<Badge>Default</Badge>
<Badge variant="success">Active</Badge>
<Badge variant="destructive">Error</Badge>
<Badge variant="warning">Pending</Badge>
<Badge variant="outline">Tag</Badge>
<Badge variant="ghost">Muted</Badge>
<Badge variant="secondary">Secondary</Badge>
```

**Variants:** `"default"` | `"secondary"` | `"destructive"` | `"success"` | `"warning"` | `"outline"` | `"ghost"`

---

### Card

Compound component with sub-components: `Card.Header`, `Card.Title`, `Card.Description`, `Card.Content`, `Card.Section`, `Card.Row`, `Card.Footer`, `Card.Layers`, `Card.LayerHeader`, `Card.LayerBody`, `Card.LayerRow`, `Card.Group`, `Card.GroupRow`.

```tsx
import { Card } from "@almach/ui";

// Standard card
<Card>
  <Card.Header action={<Badge>New</Badge>}>
    <Card.Title>Card Title</Card.Title>
    <Card.Description>Optional description text.</Card.Description>
  </Card.Header>
  <Card.Content>Main content goes here.</Card.Content>
  <Card.Section>A bordered section below content.</Card.Section>
  <Card.Row action={<ChevronRightIcon />} onClick={() => {}}>
    Clickable row
  </Card.Row>
  <Card.Footer>
    <Button>Action</Button>
  </Card.Footer>
</Card>

// Layered card (Claude/macOS-style stacked panels)
<Card.Layers>
  <Card.LayerHeader action={<Badge>Live</Badge>}>Settings</Card.LayerHeader>
  <Card.LayerBody>Content in a layer.</Card.LayerBody>
  <Card.LayerRow action={<Switch />}>Enable feature</Card.LayerRow>
</Card.Layers>
```

**Card.Header Props:** `action?: React.ReactNode` ŌĆö right-side element (icon, badge, button).
**Card.Row Props:** `action?: React.ReactNode`, standard div props. Adds hover styles when `onClick` is provided.

---

### Alert

```tsx
import { Alert } from "@almach/ui";

<Alert>
  <Alert.Body>
    <Alert.Title>Heads up!</Alert.Title>
    <Alert.Description>You can change this in settings.</Alert.Description>
  </Alert.Body>
</Alert>
<Alert variant="destructive">
  <Alert.Body>
    <Alert.Title>Error</Alert.Title>
    <Alert.Description>Something went wrong.</Alert.Description>
  </Alert.Body>
</Alert>
```

**Variants:** `"default"` | `"destructive"` | `"success"` | `"warning"`

**Sub-components:** `Alert.Icon` (icon slot), `Alert.Body` (text column), `Alert.Title`, `Alert.Description`

---

### Avatar

```tsx
import { Avatar } from "@almach/ui";

<Avatar>
  <Avatar.Image src="/avatar.png" alt="User" />
  <Avatar.Fallback>AB</Avatar.Fallback>
</Avatar>

// Size variants
<Avatar size="sm" />
<Avatar size="default" />
<Avatar size="lg" />
<Avatar size="xl" />
```

**Sizes:** `"sm"` | `"default"` | `"lg"` | `"xl"`

---

### Checkbox

```tsx
import { Checkbox } from "@almach/ui";

<Checkbox id="terms" />
<Checkbox id="terms" isSelected={checked} onChange={setChecked} />
<Checkbox id="terms" defaultSelected />
<Checkbox id="terms" isDisabled />
```

---

### Radio

```tsx
import { Radio } from "@almach/ui";

<Radio value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <Radio.Item value="a" id="a" />
  <Radio.Item value="b" id="b" />
</Radio>
```

---

### Switch

```tsx
import { Switch } from "@almach/ui";

<Switch isSelected={enabled} onChange={setEnabled} />
<Switch defaultSelected />
<Switch size="sm" />
<Switch size="lg" />
```

---

### Select

```tsx
import { Select } from "@almach/ui";

<Select value={value} onValueChange={setValue}>
  <Select.Trigger>
    <Select.Value placeholder="Choose..." />
  </Select.Trigger>
  <Select.Content>
    <Select.Item value="a">Option A</Select.Item>
    <Select.Item value="b">Option B</Select.Item>
  </Select.Content>
</Select>
```

**Sub-components:** `Select.Trigger`, `Select.Value`, `Select.Content`, `Select.Item`, `Select.Label`, `Select.Group`, `Select.Separator`, `Select.Searchable`

---

### Textarea

```tsx
import { Textarea } from "@almach/ui";

<Textarea placeholder="Write something..." rows={4} />
```

---

### Label

```tsx
import { Label } from "@almach/ui";

<Label htmlFor="email">Email address</Label>
```

---

### Tabs

```tsx
import { Tabs } from "@almach/ui";

<Tabs defaultSelectedKey="overview">
  <Tabs.List>
    <Tabs.Trigger id="overview">Overview</Tabs.Trigger>
    <Tabs.Trigger id="settings">Settings</Tabs.Trigger>
  </Tabs.List>
  <Tabs.Content id="overview">Overview content</Tabs.Content>
  <Tabs.Content id="settings">Settings content</Tabs.Content>
</Tabs>

// Variants (pass to Tabs.List)
<Tabs.List variant="underline">
  <Tabs.Trigger id="a">Tab A</Tabs.Trigger>
</Tabs.List>
```

**List variants:** `"pill"` (default) | `"underline"` | `"minimal"`

---

### Dialog

```tsx
import { Dialog } from "@almach/ui";

<Dialog>
  <Dialog.Trigger asChild>
    <Button>Open</Button>
  </Dialog.Trigger>
  <Dialog.Content>
    <Dialog.Header>
      <Dialog.Title>Are you sure?</Dialog.Title>
      <Dialog.Description>This action cannot be undone.</Dialog.Description>
    </Dialog.Header>
    <Dialog.Footer>
      <Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button>
    </Dialog.Footer>
  </Dialog.Content>
</Dialog>
```

**Sub-components:** `Dialog.Trigger`, `Dialog.Content`, `Dialog.Overlay`, `Dialog.Header`, `Dialog.Footer`, `Dialog.Title`, `Dialog.Description`, `Dialog.Close`

---

### Drawer

```tsx
import { Drawer } from "@almach/ui";

<Drawer>
  <Drawer.Trigger asChild>
    <Button>Open Drawer</Button>
  </Drawer.Trigger>
  <Drawer.Content>
    <Drawer.Header>
      <Drawer.Title>Settings</Drawer.Title>
    </Drawer.Header>
    {/* content */}
  </Drawer.Content>
</Drawer>
```

**Sub-components:** `Drawer.Trigger`, `Drawer.Content`, `Drawer.Overlay`, `Drawer.Header`, `Drawer.Footer`, `Drawer.Title`, `Drawer.Description`, `Drawer.Close`

---

### Modal

Responsive modal ŌĆö dialog on desktop, bottom sheet on mobile. Supports both a simple regular mode and a family multi-view mode (animated view switching with height transitions) via a single `views` prop.

```tsx
import { Modal, useModal } from "@almach/ui";
import type { ViewsRegistry } from "@almach/ui";

// ŌöĆŌöĆ Regular modal ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ
// Shorthand: trigger + title as props
<Modal
  trigger={<Button>Open</Button>}
  title="Confirm deletion"
  description="This action cannot be undone."
>
  <Modal.Footer>
    <Modal.Close asChild><Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button></Modal.Close>
    <Button variant="destructive">Delete</Button>
  </Modal.Footer>
</Modal>

// Controlled (no trigger needed)
<Modal open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen} title="Settings">
  <p>Settings content</p>
  <Modal.Footer><Button onClick={save}>Save</Button></Modal.Footer>
</Modal>

// Compound (full control)
<Modal>
  <Modal.Trigger asChild><Button>Open</Button></Modal.Trigger>
  <Modal.Content>
    <Modal.Header>
      <Modal.Title>Custom title</Modal.Title>
      <Modal.Description>Custom description.</Modal.Description>
    </Modal.Header>
    <p>Body</p>
    <Modal.Footer>
      <Modal.Close asChild><Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button></Modal.Close>
      <Button>Confirm</Button>
    </Modal.Footer>
  </Modal.Content>
</Modal>

// ŌöĆŌöĆ Family multi-view modal ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ
// Define views ŌĆö call useModal() inside each to navigate
function DefaultView() {
  const { setView } = useModal();
  return (
    <>
      <header className="mb-4 flex h-[72px] items-center border-b border-border pl-2">
        <h2 className="text-[19px] font-semibold">Menu</h2>
      </header>
      <div className="space-y-3">
        <Modal.NavButton onClick={() => setView("settings")}>Settings</Modal.NavButton>
        <Modal.NavButton onClick={() => setView("profile")}>Profile</Modal.NavButton>
      </div>
    </>
  );
}

function SettingsView() {
  const { setView } = useModal();
  return (
    <div>
      <Modal.ViewHeader title="Settings" description="Configure your preferences." />
      <div className="mt-7 flex gap-4">
        <Modal.ActionButton onClick={() => setView("default")} className="bg-secondary text-secondary-foreground">
          Back
        </Modal.ActionButton>
        <Modal.ActionButton onClick={() => setView("default")} className="bg-primary text-primary-foreground">
          Save
        </Modal.ActionButton>
      </div>
    </div>
  );
}

const views: ViewsRegistry = { default: DefaultView, settings: SettingsView };

// Shorthand: trigger prop auto-wires everything
<Modal views={views} trigger={<Button>Open</Button>} />

// Compound family mode
<Modal views={views}>
  <Modal.Trigger asChild><Button>Open</Button></Modal.Trigger>
  <Modal.Content />
</Modal>

// Controlled family mode
<Modal views={views} open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen} defaultView="settings" />
```

**Root Props:**

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `trigger` | `ReactNode` | ŌĆö | Shorthand: wraps element in Modal.Trigger |
| `title` | `string` | ŌĆö | Shorthand: renders Modal.Header + Modal.Title (regular mode) |
| `description` | `string` | ŌĆö | Shorthand: renders Modal.Description below the title |
| `views` | `ViewsRegistry` | ŌĆö | Enables family mode: map of view name ŌåÆ component |
| `defaultView` | `string` | `"default"` | Starting view for family mode |
| `onViewChange` | `(view: string) => void` | ŌĆö | Called when the active view changes |
| `open` | `boolean` | ŌĆö | Controlled open state |
| `defaultOpen` | `boolean` | `false` | Uncontrolled default open |
| `onOpenChange` | `(open: boolean) => void` | ŌĆö | Called when open state changes |

**Sub-components:**

| Component | Mode | Description |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| `Modal.Trigger` | both | Opens the modal ŌĆö use `asChild` to compose |
| `Modal.Content` | both | Container. With no children in family mode, auto-renders everything |
| `Modal.Header` | regular | Styled header div |
| `Modal.Title` | regular | Modal title |
| `Modal.Description` | regular | Modal description |
| `Modal.Footer` | regular | Footer div with responsive layout |
| `Modal.Close` | both | Close button ŌĆö use `asChild` to wrap your own element |
| `Modal.ViewHeader` | family | Header with optional `icon`, `title`, `description` |
| `Modal.NavButton` | family | Full-width muted navigation button |
| `Modal.ActionButton` | family | Full-width pill action button (`className` required for color) |
| `Modal.ViewContent` | family | Renders the active view (advanced) |
| `Modal.AnimatedWrapper` | family | Measures content height (advanced) |
| `Modal.AnimatedContent` | family | Handles fade+scale view transitions (advanced) |

**`useModal` hook** ŌĆö call inside any view component to navigate:
```tsx
const { view, setView } = useModal();
```

**`ViewsRegistry` type:** `{ [viewName: string]: React.ComponentType<Record<string, unknown>> }`

---

### FamilyModal

Desktop-only dialog with animated multi-view switching and smooth height transitions. For a responsive variant with multi-view support, use `Modal` with a `views` prop instead.

```tsx
import { FamilyModal, useFamilyModal } from "@almach/ui";

function HomeView() {
  const { setView } = useFamilyModal();
  return (
    <>
      <FamilyModal.Header title="Menu" description="Choose an option." />
      <div className="mt-5 space-y-2">
        <FamilyModal.Button onClick={() => setView("settings")}>Settings</FamilyModal.Button>
      </div>
    </>
  );
}

function SettingsView() {
  const { setView } = useFamilyModal();
  return (
    <>
      <FamilyModal.Header title="Settings" description="Manage preferences." />
      <FamilyModal.SecondaryButton onClick={() => setView("default")} className="text-muted-foreground">
        ŌåÉ Back
      </FamilyModal.SecondaryButton>
    </>
  );
}

const views = { default: HomeView, settings: SettingsView };

<FamilyModal views={views}>
  <FamilyModal.Trigger asChild>
    <Button>Open</Button>
  </FamilyModal.Trigger>
  <FamilyModal.Content />
</FamilyModal>

// Controlled
<FamilyModal open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen} views={views} defaultView="default" />
```

**Props:** `views` (required), `defaultView`, `open`, `onOpenChange`, `onViewChange`

**Sub-components:** `FamilyModal.Trigger`, `FamilyModal.Content`, `FamilyModal.Close`, `FamilyModal.Header` (`icon?`, `title`, `description?`), `FamilyModal.Button`, `FamilyModal.SecondaryButton`

**`useFamilyModal` hook** ŌĆö call inside any view component:
```tsx
const { view, setView } = useFamilyModal();
```

---

### Popover

```tsx
import { Popover } from "@almach/ui";

<Popover>
  <Popover.Trigger asChild>
    <Button variant="outline">Options</Button>
  </Popover.Trigger>
  <Popover.Content>Popover content here.</Popover.Content>
</Popover>
```

**Sub-components:** `Popover.Trigger`, `Popover.Content`, `Popover.Anchor`, `Popover.Close`

---

### DropdownMenu

```tsx
import { DropdownMenu } from "@almach/ui";

<DropdownMenu>
  <DropdownMenu.Trigger asChild>
    <Button variant="ghost"><MoreHorizontalIcon /></Button>
  </DropdownMenu.Trigger>
  <DropdownMenu.Content align="end">
    <DropdownMenu.Item>Edit</DropdownMenu.Item>
    <DropdownMenu.Item>Duplicate</DropdownMenu.Item>
    <DropdownMenu.Separator />
    <DropdownMenu.Item className="text-destructive">Delete</DropdownMenu.Item>
  </DropdownMenu.Content>
</DropdownMenu>
```

**Sub-components:** `DropdownMenu.Trigger`, `DropdownMenu.Content`, `DropdownMenu.Item`, `DropdownMenu.CheckboxItem`, `DropdownMenu.RadioItem`, `DropdownMenu.RadioGroup`, `DropdownMenu.Label`, `DropdownMenu.Separator`, `DropdownMenu.Shortcut`, `DropdownMenu.Sub`, `DropdownMenu.SubTrigger`, `DropdownMenu.SubContent`

---

### Command

```tsx
import { Command } from "@almach/ui";

<Command>
  <Command.Input placeholder="Search..." />
  <Command.List>
    <Command.Empty>No results found.</Command.Empty>
    <Command.Group heading="Suggestions">
      <Command.Item>Calendar</Command.Item>
      <Command.Item>Settings</Command.Item>
    </Command.Group>
  </Command.List>
</Command>
```

**Sub-components:** `Command.Input`, `Command.List`, `Command.Empty`, `Command.Group`, `Command.Item`, `Command.Separator`, `Command.Shortcut`, `Command.Dialog`

---

### Select.Searchable (Combobox)

Searchable select with a popover and built-in search input. Replaces the standalone Combobox.

```tsx
import { Select } from "@almach/ui";

const options = [
  { label: "React", value: "react" },
  { label: "Vue", value: "vue" },
];

<Select.Searchable
  options={options}
  value={value}
  onChange={setValue}
  placeholder="Select frameworkŌĆ”"
  searchPlaceholder="SearchŌĆ”"
/>
```

---

### Calendar

```tsx
import { Calendar } from "@almach/ui";

<Calendar
  mode="single"
  selected={date}
  onSelect={setDate}
  defaultMonth={new Date()}
/>
```

---

### Table

```tsx
import { Table } from "@almach/ui";

// Manual table
<Table>
  <Table.Header>
    <Table.Row>
      <Table.Head>Name</Table.Head>
      <Table.Head>Status</Table.Head>
    </Table.Row>
  </Table.Header>
  <Table.Body>
    <Table.Row>
      <Table.Cell>Alice</Table.Cell>
      <Table.Cell><Badge variant="success">Active</Badge></Table.Cell>
    </Table.Row>
  </Table.Body>
</Table>

// Data table (TanStack Table powered)
import type { ColumnDef } from "@almach/ui";

const columns: ColumnDef<User>[] = [
  { accessorKey: "name", header: "Name" },
  { accessorKey: "status", header: "Status" },
];

<Table.Data columns={columns} data={users} />
```

**Sub-components:** `Table.Header`, `Table.Body`, `Table.Row`, `Table.Head`, `Table.Cell`, `Table.Caption`, `Table.Data`

---

### SwipeActions

Reveal contextual actions by swiping in any direction ŌĆö left, right, up, or down. Works with both touch and mouse (pointer events). Text selection is disabled during drag. No external dependencies.

```tsx
import { SwipeActions } from "@almach/ui";

// Right slot ŌĆö revealed by swiping left
<SwipeActions>
  <SwipeActions.Right>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="secondary" onClick={archive}>
      <Archive /> Archive
    </SwipeActions.Action>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="destructive" onClick={remove}>
      <Trash2 /> Delete
    </SwipeActions.Action>
  </SwipeActions.Right>
  <SwipeActions.Content className="bg-background px-4 py-3">
    {/* row content */}
  </SwipeActions.Content>
</SwipeActions>

// Left + Right slots (swipe right = complete, swipe left = delete)
<SwipeActions>
  <SwipeActions.Left>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="success" onClick={complete}>
      <Check /> Done
    </SwipeActions.Action>
  </SwipeActions.Left>
  <SwipeActions.Right>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="destructive" onClick={remove}>
      <Trash2 /> Delete
    </SwipeActions.Action>
  </SwipeActions.Right>
  <SwipeActions.Content>...</SwipeActions.Content>
</SwipeActions>

// Top + Bottom slots (swipe down = top, swipe up = bottom)
<SwipeActions>
  <SwipeActions.Top>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="secondary"><Share2 /> Share</SwipeActions.Action>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="warning"><Pin /> Pin</SwipeActions.Action>
  </SwipeActions.Top>
  <SwipeActions.Bottom>
    <SwipeActions.Action variant="destructive"><Trash2 /> Delete</SwipeActions.Action>
  </SwipeActions.Bottom>
  <SwipeActions.Content>...</SwipeActions.Content>
</SwipeActions>

// All four directions
<SwipeActions>
  <SwipeActions.Left>...</SwipeActions.Left>
  <SwipeActions.Right>...</SwipeActions.Right>
  <SwipeActions.Top>...</SwipeActions.Top>
  <SwipeActions.Bottom>...</SwipeActions.Bottom>
  <SwipeActions.Content>...</SwipeActions.Content>
</SwipeActions>

// Disabled
<SwipeActions disabled>...</SwipeActions>

// Programmatic control from a child
import { useSwipeActions } from "@almach/ui";

function ActionButton() {
  const { close, open } = useSwipeActions();
  return <button onClick={close}>Dismiss</button>;
}
```

**Sub-components:** `SwipeActions.Content`, `SwipeActions.Left`, `SwipeActions.Right`, `SwipeActions.Top`, `SwipeActions.Bottom`, `SwipeActions.Action`

**Slot layout:** Left/Right use vertical button layout (icon above label). Top/Bottom use horizontal layout (icon beside label, buttons share full width).

**Action variants:** `default` ┬Ę `destructive` ┬Ę `success` ┬Ę `warning` ┬Ę `secondary`

**Props:**
- `SwipeActions`: `disabled?: boolean`, `onOpenChange?: (side: "left" | "right" | "top" | "bottom" | null) => void`
- `SwipeActions.Action`: `variant?`, `closeOnAction?: boolean` (default `true`)

**Hook:** `useSwipeActions()` ŌĆö returns `{ openSide, close, open }` for children inside `<SwipeActions>`

---

### SwipeButton

Confirm-by-swiping interaction with spring physics. Prevents accidental taps. Supports pointer, touch, and keyboard (Space/Enter).

Sub-components: `SwipeButton.Track`, `SwipeButton.Fill`, `SwipeButton.Overlay`, `SwipeButton.Thumb`, `SwipeButton.Progress`

```tsx
import { SwipeButton } from "@almach/ui";

// Basic
<SwipeButton onSuccess={() => console.log("confirmed!")} className="w-72">
  <SwipeButton.Track>
    <SwipeButton.Fill />
    <SwipeButton.Overlay>Swipe to confirm</SwipeButton.Overlay>
    <SwipeButton.Thumb />
  </SwipeButton.Track>
</SwipeButton>

// Destructive variant
<SwipeButton onSuccess={handleDelete} className="w-72">
  <SwipeButton.Track>
    <SwipeButton.Fill />
    <SwipeButton.Overlay>Swipe to delete</SwipeButton.Overlay>
    <SwipeButton.Thumb variant="destructive" />
  </SwipeButton.Track>
</SwipeButton>

// Hold-to-confirm (1 second)
<SwipeButton onSuccess={handleConfirm} hold={1000} className="w-72">
  <SwipeButton.Track>
    <SwipeButton.Fill />
    <SwipeButton.Overlay>Swipe + hold to confirm</SwipeButton.Overlay>
    <SwipeButton.Thumb variant="destructive" />
  </SwipeButton.Track>
</SwipeButton>

// Auto-reset after success
<SwipeButton onSuccess={onConfirm} resetOnSuccess resetDelay={800} className="w-72">
  <SwipeButton.Track>
    <SwipeButton.Fill />
    <SwipeButton.Overlay>Swipe to confirm</SwipeButton.Overlay>
    <SwipeButton.Thumb variant="success" />
  </SwipeButton.Track>
</SwipeButton>
```

**SwipeButton Root Props:**

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `onSuccess` | `() => void` | ŌĆö | Fired when swipe (+ optional hold) completes |
| `onFail` | `() => void` | ŌĆö | Fired on release without completing |
| `threshold` | `number` | `0.85` | Fraction (0ŌĆō1) of track width required |
| `hold` | `number` | `0` | Ms to hold at threshold before success. 0 = instant |
| `resetOnSuccess` | `boolean` | `false` | Thumb springs back after success |
| `resetDelay` | `number` | `1200` | Ms before reset when `resetOnSuccess` is true |
| `reverseSwipe` | `boolean` | `false` | Swipe right-to-left |
| `disabled` | `boolean` | `false` | Prevents interaction |

**SwipeButton.Thumb variant:** `"default"` | `"destructive"` | `"success"` | `"warning"`

---

### Carousel

```tsx
import { Carousel } from "@almach/ui";

<Carousel>
  <Carousel.Content>
    <Carousel.Item>Slide 1</Carousel.Item>
    <Carousel.Item>Slide 2</Carousel.Item>
  </Carousel.Content>
  <Carousel.Previous />
  <Carousel.Next />
  <Carousel.Dots />
</Carousel>
```

**Sub-components:** `Carousel.Content`, `Carousel.Item`, `Carousel.Previous`, `Carousel.Next`, `Carousel.Dots`

### Progress

```tsx
import { Progress } from "@almach/ui";

<Progress value={60} />
```

### Skeleton

```tsx
import { Skeleton } from "@almach/ui";

<Skeleton className="h-4 w-48" />
<Skeleton className="h-12 w-12 rounded-full" />
```

### Separator

```tsx
import { Separator } from "@almach/ui";

<Separator />
<Separator orientation="vertical" />
```

---

### Tooltip

```tsx
import { Tooltip } from "@almach/ui";

<Tooltip.Provider>
  <Tooltip>
    <Tooltip.Trigger asChild>
      <Button variant="ghost"><HelpCircleIcon /></Button>
    </Tooltip.Trigger>
    <Tooltip.Content>Helpful tip</Tooltip.Content>
  </Tooltip>
</Tooltip.Provider>
```

**Sub-components:** `Tooltip.Provider`, `Tooltip.Trigger`, `Tooltip.Content`

---

### Collapsible

```tsx
import { Collapsible } from "@almach/ui";

<Collapsible open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}>
  <Collapsible.Trigger asChild>
    <Button variant="ghost">Toggle</Button>
  </Collapsible.Trigger>
  <Collapsible.Content>Hidden content</Collapsible.Content>
</Collapsible>
```

**Sub-components:** `Collapsible.Trigger`, `Collapsible.Content`

---

### Card.Group

Grouped rows with an optional label and hint. Merged into Card ŌĆö use `Card.Group` and `Card.GroupRow`.

```tsx
import { Card } from "@almach/ui";

// Navigation list
<Card.Group>
  <Card.GroupRow action={<ArrowRightIcon />}>Get started</Card.GroupRow>
  <Card.GroupRow action={<ArrowRightIcon />}>Documentation</Card.GroupRow>
</Card.Group>

// Settings with label + hint
<Card.Group label="Notifications" hint="These apply to your account.">
  <Card.GroupRow
    label="Email alerts"
    description="Receive summaries and digests."
    action={<Switch defaultSelected />}
  />
  <Card.GroupRow label="Push notifications" action={<Switch />} />
</Card.Group>

// Form fields
<Card.Group label="Account">
  <Card.GroupRow label="Display name" htmlFor="name">
    <Input id="name" defaultValue="Alice" className="w-40" />
  </Card.GroupRow>
</Card.Group>
```

**Card.Group Props:** `label?: string`, `hint?: string`, plus all div props.
**Card.GroupRow Props:** `label?: string`, `description?: string`, `htmlFor?: string`, `required?: boolean`, `action?: React.ReactNode`, `onClick?: () => void`.

---

### TagInput

```tsx
import { TagInput } from "@almach/ui";

<TagInput
  value={tags}
  onChange={setTags}
  placeholder="Add tag..."
/>
```

---

### Toast & Toaster

Powered by Sonner. Add `<Toaster />` once at the app root. Call `toast()` from anywhere.

```tsx
import { Toaster, toast } from "@almach/ui";

// App root
<Toaster />

// Anywhere in your app
toast("Notification", { description: "You have a new message." });
toast.success("Saved!", { description: "Your changes were saved." });
toast.error("Error", { description: "Something failed." });
toast.warning("Warning", { description: "Please review your input." });
toast.promise(saveData(), {
  loading: "SavingŌĆ”",
  success: "Saved!",
  error: "Failed to save.",
});
```

---

### Chart

Recharts-based data visualization. Wraps `recharts` with themed defaults and a consistent API. Powered by `Chart.Container`, which applies the `CHART_COLORS` palette and wires up `ChartTooltipContent`.

Sub-components: `Chart.Container`, `Chart.Tooltip`, `Chart.TooltipContent`, `Chart.Legend`, `Chart.Grid`, `Chart.XAxis`, `Chart.YAxis`, `Chart.PolarGrid`, `Chart.PolarAxis`

```tsx
import { Chart } from "@almach/ui";
import {
  Line, LineChart,
  Bar, BarChart,
  Area, AreaChart,
  Pie, PieChart, Cell,
  Radar, RadarChart,
} from "recharts";

const data = [
  { month: "Jan", value: 186, prev: 120 },
  { month: "Feb", value: 305, prev: 200 },
  { month: "Mar", value: 237, prev: 180 },
];

// Line chart
<Chart.Container className="h-64 w-full">
  <LineChart data={data}>
    <Chart.Grid />
    <Chart.XAxis dataKey="month" />
    <Chart.YAxis />
    <Chart.Tooltip />
    <Chart.Legend />
    <Line type="monotone" dataKey="value" stroke="var(--color-chart-1)" strokeWidth={2} dot={false} />
    <Line type="monotone" dataKey="prev" stroke="var(--color-chart-2)" strokeWidth={2} dot={false} />
  </LineChart>
</Chart.Container>

// Bar chart
<Chart.Container className="h-64 w-full">
  <BarChart data={data}>
    <Chart.Grid />
    <Chart.XAxis dataKey="month" />
    <Chart.YAxis />
    <Chart.Tooltip />
    <Bar dataKey="value" fill="var(--color-chart-1)" radius={[4, 4, 0, 0]} />
  </BarChart>
</Chart.Container>

// Area chart
<Chart.Container className="h-64 w-full">
  <AreaChart data={data}>
    <Chart.Grid />
    <Chart.XAxis dataKey="month" />
    <Chart.YAxis />
    <Chart.Tooltip />
    <Area type="monotone" dataKey="value" stroke="var(--color-chart-1)" fill="var(--color-chart-1)" fillOpacity={0.15} />
  </AreaChart>
</Chart.Container>

// Pie chart
const pieData = [
  { name: "A", value: 400 },
  { name: "B", value: 300 },
  { name: "C", value: 200 },
];
<Chart.Container className="h-64 w-full">
  <PieChart>
    <Pie data={pieData} dataKey="value" nameKey="name" cx="50%" cy="50%" outerRadius={80}>
      {pieData.map((_, i) => (
        <Cell key={i} fill={`var(--color-chart-${(i % 5) + 1})`} />
      ))}
    </Pie>
    <Chart.Tooltip />
    <Chart.Legend />
  </PieChart>
</Chart.Container>

// Radar chart
const radarData = [
  { subject: "Speed", A: 120, B: 90 },
  { subject: "Power", A: 90, B: 110 },
  { subject: "Range", A: 86, B: 130 },
];
<Chart.Container className="h-64 w-full">
  <RadarChart data={radarData}>
    <Chart.PolarGrid />
    <Chart.PolarAxis dataKey="subject" />
    <Radar name="A" dataKey="A" stroke="var(--color-chart-1)" fill="var(--color-chart-1)" fillOpacity={0.4} />
    <Radar name="B" dataKey="B" stroke="var(--color-chart-2)" fill="var(--color-chart-2)" fillOpacity={0.4} />
    <Chart.Legend />
  </RadarChart>
</Chart.Container>
```

**Chart colors (CSS variables):** `--color-chart-1` through `--color-chart-5` ŌĆö override in your `:root` to match your brand.

**`Chart.Container` Props:**

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `className` | `string` | Applied to the ResponsiveContainer wrapper |
| `children` | `React.ReactNode` | A single recharts `*Chart` element |

---

### ScrollArea

Themed scrollable container that replaces the browser's native scrollbar with a thin, hover-revealed custom scrollbar. Composed of sub-components for fine-grained control.

Sub-components: `ScrollArea.Root`, `ScrollArea.Viewport`, `ScrollArea.Content`, `ScrollArea.Scrollbar` (alias: `ScrollArea.ScrollBar`), `ScrollArea.Thumb`, `ScrollArea.Corner`

```tsx
import { ScrollArea } from "@almach/ui";

// Vertical scroll (add className="group" to enable hover-show)
<ScrollArea className="group h-48 w-64 rounded-md">
  <ScrollArea.Viewport>
    <ScrollArea.Content className="p-3">
      {items.map((item) => (
        <p key={item} className="py-1 text-sm">{item}</p>
      ))}
    </ScrollArea.Content>
  </ScrollArea.Viewport>
  <ScrollArea.Scrollbar orientation="vertical">
    <ScrollArea.Thumb />
  </ScrollArea.Scrollbar>
</ScrollArea>

// Horizontal scroll
<ScrollArea className="group w-64 rounded-md">
  <ScrollArea.Viewport>
    <ScrollArea.Content className="flex gap-3 p-3" style={{ width: "max-content" }}>
      {items.map((item) => <div key={item} className="flex-shrink-0 w-20">{item}</div>)}
    </ScrollArea.Content>
  </ScrollArea.Viewport>
  <ScrollArea.Scrollbar orientation="horizontal">
    <ScrollArea.Thumb />
  </ScrollArea.Scrollbar>
</ScrollArea>

// Both axes
<ScrollArea className="group h-48 w-64 rounded-md">
  <ScrollArea.Viewport>
    <ScrollArea.Content className="p-3" style={{ width: "480px" }}>
      {/* wide content */}
    </ScrollArea.Content>
  </ScrollArea.Viewport>
  <ScrollArea.Scrollbar orientation="vertical"><ScrollArea.Thumb /></ScrollArea.Scrollbar>
  <ScrollArea.Scrollbar orientation="horizontal"><ScrollArea.Thumb /></ScrollArea.Scrollbar>
  <ScrollArea.Corner />
</ScrollArea>
```

**Notes:**
- Add `className="group"` to the root so the `group-hover:opacity-100` on `Scrollbar` works.
- `ScrollArea.Scrollbar` `orientation` prop: `"vertical"` (default) | `"horizontal"`.

---

## @almach/ui Hooks

```tsx
import { useMediaQuery, useIsMobile, useCopyToClipboard } from "@almach/ui";

// Responsive
const isDesktop = useMediaQuery("(min-width: 768px)");
const isMobile = useIsMobile(); // true when width < 768px

// Clipboard
const [copied, copy] = useCopyToClipboard();
copy("text to copy");
```

---

## @almach/forms

### useBasedForm

Opinionated wrapper around TanStack Form. Zod schemas are supported natively.

```tsx
import { useBasedForm, z } from "@almach/forms";
import { Button } from "@almach/ui";

const schema = z.object({
  email: z.string().email("Invalid email"),
  password: z.string().min(8, "Min 8 characters"),
});

function LoginForm() {
  const form = useBasedForm({
    defaultValues: { email: "", password: "" },
    validators: { onChange: schema },
    onSubmit: async ({ value }) => {
      await login(value);
    },
  });

  return (
    <form
      onSubmit={(e) => {
        e.preventDefault();
        form.handleSubmit();
      }}
      className="space-y-4"
    >
      <form.AppField name="email" validators={{ onChange: z.string().email() }}>
        {() => <form.TextField label="Email" type="email" required />}
      </form.AppField>

      <form.AppField name="password">
        {() => <form.TextField label="Password" type="password" required />}
      </form.AppField>

      <form.Subscribe selector={(s) => s.isSubmitting}>
        {(isSubmitting) => (
          <Button type="submit" loading={isSubmitting} className="w-full">
            Sign in
          </Button>
        )}
      </form.Subscribe>
    </form>
  );
}
```

### Field Components

All field components must be used inside `form.AppField`:

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `form.TextField` | Text `<input>` with label, description, error |
| `form.TextareaField` | Multi-line textarea |
| `form.SelectField` | Dropdown select (requires `options` prop) |
| `form.CheckboxField` | Checkbox with label |
| `form.SwitchField` | Toggle switch with label |

**TextField / TextareaField Props:**

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | Field label |
| `description` | `string` | Help text below the field |
| `required` | `boolean` | Shows required indicator |
| All native input/textarea props | | `type`, `placeholder`, etc. |

**SelectField Props:**

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | Field label |
| `options` | `SelectOption[]` | `{ label: string; value: string }[]` |
| `placeholder` | `string` | Placeholder text |

**CheckboxField / SwitchField Props:**

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `label` | `string` | Label shown next to control |
| `description` | `string` | Help text |

### Low-level Form Primitives

Use these to build custom field layouts:

```tsx
import {
  FormItem,
  FormLabel,
  FormControl,
  FormDescription,
  FormMessage,
  useFormField,
} from "@almach/forms";

function CustomField({ label, description }: { label: string; description?: string }) {
  const { error } = useFormField();
  return (
    <FormItem>
      <FormLabel>{label}</FormLabel>
      <FormControl>
        <input className="..." />
      </FormControl>
      {description && <FormDescription>{description}</FormDescription>}
      <FormMessage />
    </FormItem>
  );
}
```

### Field Context Hooks

```tsx
import { useFieldContext, useFormContext } from "@almach/forms";

// Inside a field component:
const field = useFieldContext<string>(); // gives .state.value, .handleChange, .handleBlur, .state.meta
const form = useFormContext(); // gives the full form API
```

---

## @almach/query

### BasedQueryProvider

```tsx
import { BasedQueryProvider } from "@almach/query";

// Defaults:
// - staleTime: 60_000 (1 minute)
// - gcTime: 300_000 (5 minutes)
// - retry: 3 (skips retry on 4xx errors)
// - RSC-compatible (no hydration boundary needed separately)

export default function RootLayout({ children }) {
  return <BasedQueryProvider>{children}</BasedQueryProvider>;
}
```

### createQuery

```tsx
import { createQuery, useQuery } from "@almach/query";

// Define once
export const userQuery = createQuery({
  queryKey: (id: string) => ["users", id],
  queryFn: (id) => fetch(`/api/users/${id}`).then((r) => r.json()),
  staleTime: 30_000, // optional
});

// Use anywhere
const { data, isLoading, error } = useQuery(userQuery.options(userId));

// Prefetch (server)
await queryClient.prefetchQuery(userQuery.options(userId));
```

### createMutation

```tsx
import { createMutation } from "@almach/query";

export const useCreateUser = createMutation({
  mutationFn: (data: CreateUserInput) =>
    fetch("/api/users", { method: "POST", body: JSON.stringify(data) }).then((r) => r.json()),
  invalidates: [["users"]], // auto-invalidated on success
  onSuccess: (data) => {
    toast({ title: "User created!" });
  },
});

// In component:
const createUser = useCreateUser();
createUser.mutate({ name: "Alice", email: "alice@example.com" });
```

### createServerAction

For Next.js / React Server Actions:

```tsx
import { createServerAction } from "@almach/query";
import { createPostAction } from "./actions";

export function useCreatePost() {
  return createServerAction({
    action: createPostAction,         // Server Action returning ActionResult<T>
    invalidates: [["posts"]],
    onSuccess: (data) => router.push(`/posts/${data.id}`),
    onError: (error) => toast({ title: error.message, variant: "destructive" }),
  });
}
```

Server action must return `ActionResult<T>`:

```ts
type ActionResult<T> =
  | { success: true; data: T }
  | { success: false; error: ActionError };

interface ActionError {
  code?: string;
  message: string;
  fields?: Record<string, string[]>; // field-level validation errors
}
```

### useInvalidate

```tsx
import { useInvalidate } from "@almach/query";

const invalidate = useInvalidate(["users"], ["posts"]);
// Call to manually refetch
await invalidate();
```

### useOptimisticUpdate

```tsx
import { useOptimisticUpdate, useMutation } from "@almach/query";

const { optimisticUpdate } = useOptimisticUpdate<Todo[]>(["todos"]);

const mutation = useMutation({
  mutationFn: updateTodo,
  onMutate: async (newTodo) =>
    optimisticUpdate((prev) =>
      prev?.map((t) => (t.id === newTodo.id ? { ...t, ...newTodo } : t))
    ),
  onError: (_err, _vars, context) => context?.rollback(),
  onSettled: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ["todos"] }),
});
```

### TanStack Query re-exports

Everything from `@tanstack/react-query` is re-exported for convenience:

```tsx
import {
  useQuery,
  useMutation,
  useQueryClient,
  useInfiniteQuery,
  useSuspenseQuery,
  QueryClient,
  QueryClientProvider,
  keepPreviousData,
  infiniteQueryOptions,
  queryOptions,
} from "@almach/query";
```

---

## @almach/utils

### cn ŌĆö Merge class names

```tsx
import { cn } from "@almach/utils";

cn("px-4 py-2", condition && "bg-primary", className);
// Uses clsx + tailwind-merge (handles Tailwind class conflicts)
```

### formatDate

```tsx
import { formatDate } from "@almach/utils";

formatDate(new Date());                          // "Mar 20, 2026"
formatDate(new Date(), { dateStyle: "full" });   // "Friday, March 20, 2026"
formatDate(new Date(), undefined, "fr-FR");      // "20 mars 2026"
```

### formatRelativeDate

```tsx
import { formatRelativeDate } from "@almach/utils";

formatRelativeDate(new Date(Date.now() - 3600_000)); // "1 hour ago"
formatRelativeDate(new Date(Date.now() - 86400_000)); // "yesterday"
```

### URL utilities

```tsx
import { createUrl, parseSearchParams, toSearchParams } from "@almach/utils";

createUrl("/users", { page: "1", sort: "name" });
// "/users?page=1&sort=name"

parseSearchParams(new URLSearchParams("page=1&sort=name"));
// { page: "1", sort: "name" }

toSearchParams({ page: "1", sort: "name" });
// URLSearchParams { page ŌåÆ "1", sort ŌåÆ "name" }
```

### TypeScript type helpers

```tsx
import type {
  Prettify,           // Flatten intersections for readable IDE display
  NonNullableFields,  // Make all fields non-nullable
  PartialBy,          // Make specific keys optional: PartialBy<User, "avatar">
  RequiredBy,         // Make specific keys required: RequiredBy<User, "id">
  ValueOf,            // Get union of object values: ValueOf<typeof STATUS>
  Entries,            // Typed Object.entries: Entries<typeof obj>
  MaybeArray,         // T | T[]
  MaybePromise,       // T | Promise<T>
} from "@almach/utils";
```

---

## Monorepo Development

```bash
# Install all dependencies
bun install

# Build all packages
bun run build

# Run docs site (Astro)
bun run docs

# Type-check all packages
bun run typecheck

# Lint all packages
bun run lint

# Clean build artifacts
bun run clean
```

### Releasing

```bash
# 1. Create a changeset describing your change
bun changeset

# 2. Push ŌĆö CI opens a "Version Packages" PR automatically
# 3. Merge that PR ŌåÆ all changed packages publish to npm
```

### Architecture

```
almach-lab/base/
Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ packages/
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ ui/      ŌĆö @almach/ui      (React Aria Components + Tailwind)
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ forms/   ŌĆö @almach/forms   (TanStack Form + Zod fields)
Ōöé   Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ query/   ŌĆö @almach/query   (TanStack Query factories)
Ōöé   ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ utils/   ŌĆö @almach/utils   (cn, formatters, type helpers)
ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ apps/
    ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ docs/    ŌĆö Astro docs (deployed to Cloudflare Pages)
```

### Package dependency graph

```
@almach/utils  (no internal deps)
    Ōåæ
    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ @almach/ui     (depends on utils)
    Ōö£ŌöĆŌöĆ @almach/forms  (depends on ui, utils)
    ŌööŌöĆŌöĆ @almach/query  (depends on utils)
```
